title: ShaderForge — Live GLSL Playground
description: Write & run GLSL fragment shaders live — 8 presets, 4 uniform sliders, screenshot export, and shareable URL.
concept: A full-featured WebGL fragment shader editor. Pick from 8 built-in presets (Plasma, Voronoi, Mandelbrot, Raymarcher, Flow Field, Domain Warp, Galaxy, Fractal IFS), tweak four continuous uniform sliders (uA–uD), then edit the GLSL source directly with real-time compilation. The canvas updates within 600ms of your last keystroke and error messages appear inline. Move your mouse over the canvas to drive the built-in uMouse uniform, screenshot any frame as PNG, or share your shader via a base64-encoded URL hash.
features: 8 built-in GLSL presets covering plasma, fractals, voronoi, ray-marching, flow fields, domain warp, galaxy & IFS
Live GLSL editor with line numbers, Tab indent, and Ctrl+Enter compile shortcut
4 real-time uniform sliders (uA–uD) with reset buttons
URL hash sharing — full shader code encoded as base64 in the fragment
localStorage persistence of last shader and uniform values
Aspect ratio switcher (1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3) with live FPS counter

interaction.mouse: Move over canvas to drive uMouse uniform (vec2, 0–1)
interaction.click: Preset buttons load new shaders; Run recompiles; Screenshot exports PNG
interaction.touch: Touch drag over canvas updates mouse uniform
interaction.keyboard: Tab inserts 2-space indent; Ctrl+Enter compiles immediately
